question_text: A student heats ice at 0°C continuously. During the first part of heating, the temperature remains at 0°C even though energy is being added. Explain why the temperature does not change during this phase, and state the name of this process.
correct_answer: This is melting. The energy added is used to break the bonds holding particles in fixed positions, not to increase their kinetic energy, so temperature stays constant until all the ice has melted.
explanation: During a phase change, energy overcomes intermolecular forces rather than increasing particle speed, so temperature remains constant. Once melting is complete, further heating increases the temperature of the liquid water.
